name: RHYTHM PHARMACEUTICALS, INC.
business: Rhythm Pharmaceuticals is a global, commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on advancing melanocortin-4 receptor (MC4R) agonists, including its lead asset IMCIVREE (setmelanotide), as precision medicines to treat hyperphagia and severe obesity caused by rare MC4R pathway diseases. IMCIVREE is approved for certain rare MC4R pathway diseases including Bardet-Biedl syndrome, and is under review for acquired hypothalamic obesity. The company also develops earlier-stage investigational MC4R agonists bivamelagon and RM-718.
sellsTo: patients with rare MC4R pathway diseases

exposures.sentence: We are a global, commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company dedicated to transforming the lives of patients living with rare neuroendocrine diseases. We are focused on advancing melanocortin-4 receptor (MC4R) agonists, including our lead asset, IMCIVREE (setmelanotide), as precision medicines designed to treat hyperphagia and severe obesity caused by rare MC4R pathway diseases.

catalysts.event: FDA PDUFA goal date for setmelanotide in acquired hypothalamic obesity
catalysts.sentence: Reviews of our regulatory submissions seeking approval and marketing authorization for setmelanotide as a treatment for acquired hypothalamic obesity are ongoing in the United States and Europe, with an FDA-assigned Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) goal date of March 20, 2026.

catalysts.event: Topline data from Japanese cohort of Phase 3 trial in acquired hypothalamic obesity
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate disclosing topline data from a 12-patient Japanese cohort of our Phase 3 trial evaluating setmelanotide for acquired hypothalamic obesity in March 2026, and pending positive data, completing a new drug application submission in Japan.

catalysts.event: Topline data from Phase 3 EMANATE trial substudies
catalysts.sentence: Our Phase 3 EMANATE trial, comprised of four independent substudies evaluating setmelanotide in genetically caused MC4R pathway diseases, is ongoing with topline data expected in March 2026.

catalysts.event: Initiation of Phase 3 registrational trial for bivamelagon in hypothalamic obesity
catalysts.sentence: Also, we plan to initiate a Phase 3 registrational trial to evaluate bivamelagon in patients with hypothalamic obesity by year-end 2026.

catalysts.event: Completion of enrollment in congenital hypothalamic obesity substudy
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate completing enrollment in this substudy in congenital hypothalamic obesity, which is independent from the pivotal Phase 3 trial cohort in acquired hypothalamic obesity, in the second half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 10,000 patients in the United States with acquired hypothalamic obesity
tamClaims.sentence: We believe there are approximately 10,000 patients in United States living with acquired hypothalamic obesity with an annual incidence of 500 new cases, with similar prevalence and incidence in Europe, as well as between 5,000 and 8,000 patients in Japan.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 4,600 to 7,500 U.S. patients in initial FDA-approved indications (BBS and biallelic POMC/PCSK1/LEPR deficiencies)
tamClaims.sentence: Our epidemiology estimates are approximately 4,600 to 7,500 for U.S. patients in initial FDA-approved indications, including obesity due to BBS and biallelic POMC, PCSK1 or LEPR deficiencies.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 29,000 U.S. patients with SH2B1 or POMC/PCSK1 insufficiency (potential responders to setmelanotide)
tamClaims.sentence: Our epidemiology estimates for the SH2B1 insufficiency and heterozygous POMC/PCSK1 insufficiency being studied in our Phase 3 EMANATE trial suggest that approximately 29,000 U.S. patients with one of these genetically driven obesities have the potential to respond well to setmelanotide.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 65,300 U.S. patients with genetic indications from Phase 2 DAYBREAK trial
tamClaims.sentence: Similarly, our epidemiology estimates for patients with genetic indications who demonstrated an initial response in our Phase 2 DAYBREAK trial is approximately 65,300.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 20,000 people in the United States with Prader-Willi syndrome
tamClaims.sentence: PWS is estimated to affect approximately 400,000 people worldwide and approximately 20,000 people in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: Congenital hypothalamic obesity prevalence in excess of 1,000 patients in the United States
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate the prevalence of congenital hypothalamic obesity to be in excess of 1,000 patients in the United States and in excess of 1,000 patients in Europe.
